seo_title: Can Large Language Models Introspect? A Reality Check
meta_description: A reality check on large language models' ability to introspect and report their internal states.
body_html:
No, large language models cannot reliably introspect and report their internal states. Current evidence suggests that behavioral evidence alone is insufficient to establish strong introspective claims.

Methodological Limitations

  • Models struggle to distinguish between interventions on their internal states and manipulations of the input.
  • Classifiers with only access to the input achieve equivalent performance to the model's own in-context predictions.
  • Models perform closer to chance on a relabeled control setting where they cannot rely on the semantics of the task.

Implications for Metacognitive Monitoring

The results indicate that current evidence is insufficient to establish that LLMs display metacognitive monitoring.

Reflection Learning and Self-Improvement

  • A novel pipeline called ReflectEvo demonstrates that small language models can enhance meta introspection through reflection learning.
  • The effectiveness of reflection learning is validated on several prominent open-sourced models, substantially boosting their reasoning abilities.

Quantifying Metacognitive Ability in LLMs

DMC framework decouples metacognitive ability and cognitive ability, tackling the challenge of noisy quantification caused by their coupling.
(read_time_minutes: 8)

source.title: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check
source.summary: Researchers have questioned whether large language models (LLMs) can introspect, or detect and report their own internal states. A study found that LLMs may not be able to reliably distinguish between manipulations of their internal states and anomalies in the input data. Additionally, a test where LLMs were asked to predict labels derived from their own hidden states showed that they performed similarly to classifiers with only access to the input, suggesting that they do not have privileged access to their internal representations. The study concludes that current evidence is insufficient to establish that LLMs display metacognitive monitoring.
